semantic.role: CLI command adapter for the `build` subcommand. Orchestrates build workflow by invoking the sync pipeline and runtime service.
responsibilities: Parse and validate CLI arguments for the build command and display a UI banner.
Delegate to runBuildWorkflow and runSyncWorkflow to execute build and sync steps.
Export a reusable runBuildWorkflow function for invocation by other modules (e.g., try, sync) without CLI coupling.
outOfScope: Actual compilation or build logic (handled by runtime service and sync pipeline).
invariants: Must be invoked from CLI entrypoint (though runBuildWorkflow is exportable for reuse).
Must not contain pipeline or persistence logic (rule: cli-entrypoint-separation).

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a thin CLI adapter for the build command, with a reusable workflow function to support programmatic builds from other modules.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Refactored to extract runBuildWorkflow as a separate export, enabling reuse across CLI commands and aligning with sync workflow reuse.

publicSurface.description: CLI handler for the build command; parses arguments and triggers the build workflow. Called by the CLI router after command dispatch.
Executes the build workflow (sync + build) programmatically, accepting rootDir, Config, and RuntimeService. Used by other CLI commands and services to run builds without CLI context.
Configuration interface for executeBuildCommand, defining CLI args, rootDir, config, runtimeService, and a displayUIBanner callback.

English.role: CLI command adapter for the `build` subcommand. It orchestrates the build workflow by invoking the sync pipeline and runtime service, while keeping pipeline logic separate.
responsibilities: Parse and validate CLI arguments for the build command and display a UI banner to indicate start.
Delegate to runBuildWorkflow and runSyncWorkflow to execute the build and sync steps.
Export a reusable runBuildWorkflow function that can be called by other modules (like `try`, `sync`) without requiring CLI context.
outOfScope: Actual compilation or build implementation (delegated to the runtime service and sync pipeline).
Other CLI commands or unrelated functionality.

exports.name: runBuildWorkflow
